disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Three weeks of heavy thirst and constant urination, including waking overnight to pass urine
trigger_question_examples: Has there been anything going on over the last few weeks?
Have they been drinking or weeing more than usual?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: For about three weeks they've been so thirsty, drinking bottle after bottle, and weeing constantly — even getting up in the night to go. We thought it was just the hot weather.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Unexplained rapid weight loss (~5 kg) despite an increased appetite
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They've lost about 5 kilos really quickly even though they've been eating more, not less. Their clothes are hanging off them.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Acute onset today of recurrent vomiting and diffuse abdominal pain, unable to keep fluids down
trigger_question_examples: Are they able to keep any fluids down?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Since this morning they've been vomiting again and again, their tummy hurts all over, and they can't keep even a sip of water down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rapid, deep, sighing breathing at rest (deep laboured hyperventilation)
trigger_question_examples: Is the breathing fast or deep even when they're resting?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The breathing is really fast and deep and heavy, like they've been running, but they're just lying in bed. It won't settle.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A sweet or acetone-like odour to the breath
trigger_question_examples: Does their breath smell unusual or sweet?
Have you noticed any odd smell on their breath?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it, yes — their breath smells oddly sweet, almost like nail-polish remover or fruit.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Progressive drowsiness, lethargy and reduced responsiveness
trigger_question_examples: How awake and alert are they right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They're really floppy and drowsy, hard to keep awake, and just want to lie there. It's frightening.

trigger_question_examples: Have they said things look blurry?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They said their vision has gone blurry today.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Signs of dehydration observable to a carer — sunken eyes, drowsiness, unable to maintain oral intake
trigger_question_examples: How do they look to you — their eyes, their skin?
Are they able to drink and keep anything down?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Their eyes look sunken and they seem really dried out, and they can't keep any fluid down at all.
